 {"id":"bd-21nj4","title":"[SEC-2.4] Quarantine-to-trust promotion workflow for unknown/high-risk extensions","description":"## Background
Not all extensions should execute immediately after install; trust must be staged.

## Scope
- Add quarantine state for unsigned/high-risk/unknown packages.
- Define explicit promotion workflow with user acknowledgment and policy constraints.
- Ensure quarantined extensions cannot bypass policy via indirect registration paths.

## Deliverables
- State machine for trust lifecycle: quarantined -> restricted -> trusted.
- UX + CLI hooks for promotion and rollback to quarantine.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Quarantined extensions cannot execute dangerous hostcalls.
- [ ] Promotion requires explicit operator action with audit trail.
- [ ] Demotion back to quarantine is supported and tested.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description is implemented fully with no feature loss

 {"id":"bd-21vng","title":"[SEC-2.3] Install-time static scanner with deterministic risk classifier","description":"## Background
We need early detection of suspicious extension patterns before first execution.

## Scope
- Expand static scanning for dangerous APIs/patterns and privilege-seeking behavior.
- Produce deterministic risk tiers with explainable rule hits.
- Write evidence ledger entries that can be correlated with runtime behavior later.

## Deliverables
- Scanner rulebook + tests.
- Risk classification report format consumed by policy/quarantine flows.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Scanner output is deterministic and reproducible.
- [ ] Each risk finding includes rule ID, rationale, and code location.
- [ ] Rule updates are versioned and backward-auditable.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description is implemented fully with no feature loss

 {"id":"bd-229m","title":"Feature: Compatibility gap analysis for expanded corpus","description":"Map expanded extensions to required shims/hostcalls; produce a prioritized gap matrix for unmodified compatibility.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:00:40.853214612Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T06:37:32.727581739Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T06:37:32.321979646Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analysis","compatibility","extensions"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-229m","depends_on_id":"bd-po15","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:27:57Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":2388,"issue_id":"bd-229m","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Purpose
Translate the expanded corpus into a concrete compatibility gap matrix. The goal is a prioritized, actionable list of shims/hostcalls required for unmodified compatibility.

Guiding Principles
- No per-extension exceptions: all compatibility gaps map to general-purpose shims or hostcalls.
- Prioritize Tier-1 coverage and high-frequency Node APIs (fs, path, child_process, process, Buffer, URL, crypto).
- Keep performance + determinism constraints from EXTENSIONS.md.

Outputs
- A gap matrix by extension + API.
- A prioritized list of shim tasks, linked to existing workstreams where possible.
","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:12:41Z"},{"id":2389,"issue_id":"bd-229m","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Closed. Gap analysis completed: 36 failures classified in conformance_baseline.json — 22 manifest mismatches, 5 missing npm stubs, 4 multi-file deps, 4 runtime errors, 1 test fixture. Actionable gaps documented in COMPATIBILITY_SUMMARY.md §Failure Classification.","created_at":"2026-02-07T06:37:32Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-229v","title":"E2E: Extension Registration lifecycle with detailed JSONL logging","description":"End-to-end test script for extension registration APIs with comprehensive logging.

## Scenario: Full Registration Lifecycle

1. Load test extension that registers:
   - 2 slash commands (/ext-hello, /ext-echo)
   - 1 keyboard shortcut (Ctrl+E)
   - 1 CLI flag (--ext-verbose)
   - 1 custom provider (mock-provider)

2. Verify registrations:
   - /ext-hello appears in autocomplete
   - /ext-hello executes JS handler
   - Shortcut triggers handler
   - --ext-verbose parsed from CLI
   - mock-provider in model list

## Logging Requirements

### JSONL Format
Each line:
```json
{"ts": "...", "level": "DEBUG", "component": "extension", "event": "...", "data": {...}}
```

### Required Events to Log
- extension_load_start
- hostcall_register_command{name, description}
- hostcall_register_shortcut{key, description}
- hostcall_register_flag{name, type}
- hostcall_register_provider{name, api}
- extension_load_complete{tools, commands, shortcuts, flags, providers}
- command_execute_start{name, args}
- command_execute_complete{name, result}

### Artifact Output
- logs/e2e_registration_{timestamp}.jsonl
- Screenshots for TUI verification (optional)

## Test Script Location
tests/e2e/extension_registration.rs","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:19:20.680855819Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T05:25:12.617417560Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T05:25:12.617350906Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-229v","depends_on_id":"bd-1yh7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:27:57Z","created_by":"import"}]}

- I/O patterns: filesystem-heavy, network-heavy, CPU-heavy.

Output:
- A sampling matrix/table that maps each candidate extension to axes, with quotas per axis.

 {"id":"bd-9sa","title":"Implement asupersync HTTP client wrapper","description":"# Implement asupersync HTTP client wrapper

## Goal
Create a functional HTTP client in src/http/client.rs using asupersync APIs.

## Current State
src/http/client.rs has:
- Client struct (empty)
- RequestBuilder struct with method/url/headers/body
- RequestBuilder::send() returns Err("asupersync HTTP client not yet implemented")
- RequestBuilder::json() silently ignores serialization errors

## Implementation Requirements

### Client API
```rust
use asupersync::{Cx, http::client::HttpClient};
use asupersync::tls::TlsConnectorBuilder;

pub struct Client {
    inner: HttpClient,
}

impl Client {
    pub fn new() -> Result<Self, Error> {
        // Create TLS connector with native roots
        let tls = TlsConnectorBuilder::new()
            .with_native_roots()?
            .build()?;
        let inner = HttpClient::new(tls);
        Ok(Self { inner })
    }
    
    pub fn request(&self, method: Method, url: &str) -> RequestBuilder {
        RequestBuilder::new(self, method, url)
    }
    
    pub fn get(&self, url: &str) -> RequestBuilder { ... }
    pub fn post(&self, url: &str) -> RequestBuilder { ... }
}
```

### RequestBuilder API
```rust
impl RequestBuilder {
    pub fn header(mut self, key: &str, value: &str) -> Self { ... }
    pub fn json<T: Serialize>(mut self, body: &T) -> Self { ... }
    pub fn body(mut self, bytes: Vec<u8>) -> Self { ... }
    pub fn timeout(mut self, duration: Duration) -> Self { ... }
    
    pub async fn send(self, cx: &Cx) -> Result<Response, Error> {
        // Actually send the request using asupersync
    }
}
```

### Response API
```rust
pub struct Response {
    status: StatusCode,
    headers: Headers,
    body: BodyReader,  // Async streaming body
}

impl Response {
    pub fn status(&self) -> StatusCode { ... }
    pub fn headers(&self) -> &Headers { ... }
    pub async fn text(self, cx: &Cx) -> Result<String, Error> { ... }
    pub async fn json<T: DeserializeOwned>(self, cx: &Cx) -> Result<T, Error> { ... }
    pub fn bytes_stream(self) -> impl Stream<Item = Result<Bytes, Error>> { ... }
}
```

## Key Considerations
1. **TLS**: Use rustls with native root certificates (no openssl)
2. **Timeout**: Support per-request timeout via asupersync Budget
3. **Streaming**: Response body must support async streaming for SSE
4. **Headers**: Support standard headers (Content-Type, Authorization, etc.)
5. **Error handling**: Wrap asupersync errors in pi's Error type

## Testing
- Unit test: Client creation succeeds
- Unit test: RequestBuilder builds correct request
- Integration test: GET request to httpbin.org/get
- Integration test: POST with JSON body
- Integration test: Timeout behavior

## Files
- src/http/client.rs (modify)
- src/http/mod.rs (update re-exports)
- src/error.rs (add HTTP error variants)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Client::new() creates working HTTP client
- [ ] GET/POST requests work
- [ ] JSON body serialization works
- [ ] Response streaming works
- [ ] Timeout respected
- [ ] TLS verification works
- [ ] All unit tests pass","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:32:01.120130883Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:24:57.792527506Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T17:12:08.576706438Z","close_reason":"Completed (HTTP client wrapper is implemented + tests pass; see comments)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-9sa","depends_on_id":"bd-gi3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:09Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":3412,"issue_id":"bd-9sa","author":"AmberForest","text":"Taking this bead: implement asupersync HTTP client wrapper (no tokio/reqwest). Will wire streaming + TLS + timeout per spec.","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:45:33Z"},{"id":3413,"issue_id":"bd-9sa","author":"AmberForest","text":"Implemented asupersync HTTP wrapper updates (Cx-aware send + timeout + StatusCode + bytes_stream). Migrated auth + npm registry calls to wrapper with Cx::for_request.  {"id":"bd-ah1","title":"Implement session persistence cycle tests","description":"# Implement session persistence cycle tests

## Goal
Test the full session lifecycle: create → save → reload → verify integrity, with detailed logs.

## Background
Session persistence involves:
- JSONL file format (one JSON object per line)
- Header with metadata (version, cwd, timestamp)
- Message entries with parent/child relationships
- Tree structure for conversation branching
- File locking for concurrent access

Current tests cover serialization but not full I/O cycles.

## Runtime + Determinism
- Do not introduce new tokio-only tests; use `#[asupersync::test]` or a runtime wrapper.
- Use temp dirs and deterministic paths; avoid asserting exact timestamps.

## Logging Requirements
- Use TestLogger (bd-3ml).
- Log session path, header fields, message IDs, tree shape, and file sizes.
- On failure, dump the JSONL contents (redacted) and parsed header.

## Test Categories

### 1. Basic Persistence Cycle Tests
```rust
#[asupersync::test]
async fn test_session_save_and_reload() {
    let temp = TempDir::new().unwrap();
    let session_path = temp.path().join("test.jsonl");

    let mut session = Session::new("/project/path");
    session.add_user_message("Hello");
    session.add_assistant_message("Hi there!");

    session.persist(&session_path).await.unwrap();

    let loaded = Session::load(&session_path).await.unwrap();
    assert_eq!(loaded.messages().len(), 2);
}
```

### 2. Tree Structure Persistence Tests
```rust
#[asupersync::test]
async fn test_branching_persists() {
    let temp = TempDir::new().unwrap();
    let session_path = temp.path().join("test.jsonl");

    let mut session = Session::new("/project");
    let root_id = session.add_user_message("Root");
    session.add_assistant_message("Branch A");

    session.navigate_to(root_id.clone());
    session.add_assistant_message("Branch B");

    session.persist(&session_path).await.unwrap();

    let loaded = Session::load(&session_path).await.unwrap();
    let children = loaded.children_of(&root_id);
    assert_eq!(children.len(), 2);
}
```

### 3. Header Metadata Tests
```rust
#[asupersync::test]
async fn test_header_metadata_preserved() {
    let temp = TempDir::new().unwrap();
    let session_path = temp.path().join("test.jsonl");

    let mut session = Session::new("/my/project/path");
    session.set_provider("anthropic");
    session.set_model("claude-opus-4");
    session.set_thinking_level(ThinkingLevel::High);
    session.persist(&session_path).await.unwrap();

    let loaded = Session::load(&session_path).await.unwrap();
    assert_eq!(loaded.cwd(), "/my/project/path");
}
```

### 4. Entry Type Persistence Tests
- ModelChange entries
- ThinkingLevel entries
- Compaction entries
- Tool invocation entries (if serialized)

### 5. Error Handling Tests
- Missing file
- Corrupted JSONL
- Partial corruption (valid header + bad line)

### 6. Concurrent Access Tests
```rust
#[asupersync::test]
async fn test_concurrent_writes_locked() {
    let temp = TempDir::new().unwrap();
    let session_path = temp.path().join("test.jsonl");

    let session1 = Session::new("/project");
    session1.persist(&session_path).await.unwrap();

    let path = session_path.clone();
    let handle1 = spawn(async move {
        let mut s = Session::load(&path).await.unwrap();
        s.add_user_message("From task 1");
        s.persist(&path).await
    });

    let path = session_path.clone();
    let handle2 = spawn(async move {
        let mut s = Session::load(&path).await.unwrap();
        s.add_user_message("From task 2");
        s.persist(&path).await
    });

    let (r1, r2) = join(handle1, handle2);
    assert!(r1.unwrap().is_ok() || r2.unwrap().is_ok());
}
```

### 7. Directory Structure Tests
- Persist creates nested directories when needed

 {"id":"bd-anewk","title":"[PERF-4] Frame budget enforcement with degraded rendering modes","description":"## Problem

When the system is under load, the TUI continues trying to render at full fidelity 60fps, which can make the terminal unresponsive. Need automatic degradation.

## Solution: Frame Budget State Machine

### CRITICAL DESIGN: view() is &self — Interior Mutability Required

The charmed_bubbletea Model trait defines `fn view(&self) -> String`. The FrameBudget state machine transitions happen based on frame timing measured INSIDE view(). Since view() only has &self, all mutable FrameBudget fields must use Cell<T>.

Architecture:
- **FrameBudget** (Cell<T> fields) — frame-level state, updated in view(&self)
- **cpu_pressure** (plain PiApp field) — system-level state, updated in update(&mut self) via tick

```rust
#[derive(Clone, Copy, PartialEq)]
enum RenderFidelity { Normal, Degraded, Emergency }

/// Frame-level budget tracking. Uses Cell<T> because state transitions
/// occur inside view(&self) after measuring frame render time.
/// All fields are Copy types, so Cell (not RefCell) suffices.
struct FrameBudget {
    fidelity: Cell<RenderFidelity>,
    consecutive_over: Cell<u8>,   // frames exceeding budget
    consecutive_under: Cell<u8>,  // frames under budget (for recovery)
}

impl FrameBudget {
    /// Called at END of view() with the frame's render time.
    /// Updates fidelity for the NEXT frame (one-frame delay is imperceptible).
    fn record_frame(&self, frame_time: Duration) {
        let budget_us = match self.fidelity.get() {
            RenderFidelity::Normal => 16_000,    // 60fps
            RenderFidelity::Degraded => 33_000,  // 30fps
            RenderFidelity::Emergency => 66_000, // 15fps
        };
        if frame_time.as_micros() as u64 > budget_us {
            self.consecutive_under.set(0);
            let over = self.consecutive_over.get() + 1;
            self.consecutive_over.set(over);
            if over >= 3 {
                self.consecutive_over.set(0);
                let new_fidelity = match self.fidelity.get() {
                    RenderFidelity::Normal => RenderFidelity::Degraded,
                    RenderFidelity::Degraded => RenderFidelity::Emergency,
                    RenderFidelity::Emergency => RenderFidelity::Emergency,
                };
                self.fidelity.set(new_fidelity);
            }
        } else {
            self.consecutive_over.set(0);
            let under = self.consecutive_under.get() + 1;
            self.consecutive_under.set(under);
            if under >= 10 {
                self.consecutive_under.set(0);
                // Recovery with hysteresis (25% margin)
                let recovered = match self.fidelity.get() {
                    RenderFidelity::Emergency => RenderFidelity::Degraded,
                    RenderFidelity::Degraded => RenderFidelity::Normal,
                    RenderFidelity::Normal => RenderFidelity::Normal,
                };
                self.fidelity.set(recovered);
            }
        }
    }
}
```

Note: budget_us is DERIVED from fidelity, not a stored field. Fewer fields = fewer Cell operations = clearer code.

### CPU Pressure: Separate from FrameBudget

CPU pressure is a system-wide signal that changes slowly (load averages are 1-minute exponential moving averages). It is sampled in update(&mut self) via a periodic tick, NOT inside view().

```rust
// In PiApp (plain fields, updated in update(&mut self)):
cpu_pressure: PressureLevel,
cpu_pressure_last_sample: Instant,

// In update(&mut self, msg):
fn update(&mut self, msg: PiMsg) -> Cmd {
    // On every message, check if 5 seconds have elapsed
    if self.cpu_pressure_last_sample.elapsed() > Duration::from_secs(5) {
        self.cpu_pressure = sample_load_average(self.cpu_count);
        self.cpu_pressure_last_sample = Instant::now();
    }
    // ...
}

// In view(&self):
fn view(&self) -> String {
    // Combine frame budget with CPU pressure floor
    let frame_fidelity = self.frame_budget.fidelity.get();
    let effective = min(frame_fidelity, self.cpu_pressure.to_fidelity_floor());
    // Use effective fidelity for rendering decisions
    // ...
}
```

Platform-specific load average reading:
```rust
fn sample_load_average(cpu_count: usize) -> PressureLevel {
    let load = read_load_avg();  // returns Option<f64>
    match load {
        None => PressureLevel::Normal, // Non-Linux/macOS: always Normal
        Some(load) => {
            let ratio = load / cpu_count as f64;
            if ratio < 1.5 { PressureLevel::Normal }
            else if ratio < 3.0 { PressureLevel::Moderate }
            else { PressureLevel::High }
        }
    }
}

fn read_load_avg() -> Option<f64> {
    #[cfg(target_os = "linux")]
    {
        let content = std::fs::read_to_string("/proc/loadavg").ok()?;
        content.split_whitespace().next()?.parse().ok()
    }
    #[cfg(any(target_os = "macos", target_os = "freebsd"))]
    {
        let mut loadavg = [0.0f64; 1];
        let ret = unsafe { libc::getloadavg(loadavg.as_mut_ptr(), 1) };
        if ret == 1 { Some(loadavg[0]) } else { None }
    }
    #[cfg(not(any(target_os = "linux", target_os = "macos", target_os = "freebsd")))]
    { None }
}
```

### State Machine

```
Normal (60fps, full markdown)
  | 3 consecutive frames > 16ms
  v
Degraded (30fps, simplified markdown)
  | 3 consecutive frames > 33ms
  v
Emergency (15fps, raw text, no markdown)
  ^ 10 consecutive frames < budget (with 25% hysteresis margin)
```

### Degradation Levels

1. **Normal**: Full markdown rendering via glamour::render_with_word_wrap(), 60fps, all visual features
2. **Degraded**: Lightweight markdown (textwrap::fill() with bold/italic only, no syntax highlighting), effective 30fps via frame skipping (skip even-numbered frames, return cached view output)
3. **Emergency**: Raw text (no markdown at all), effective 15fps (skip 3 of 4 frames). Skip thinking blocks, minimal chrome.

### Frame Skipping Implementation
```rust
// In view(&self):
let frame_count = self.frame_timing.total_frames.get();
let fidelity = effective_fidelity;
let should_skip = match fidelity {
    RenderFidelity::Normal => false,
    RenderFidelity::Degraded => frame_count % 2 != 0,
    RenderFidelity::Emergency => frame_count % 4 != 0,
};
if should_skip {
    // Return cached last output (stored in RefCell<String>)
    return self.cached_view_output.borrow().clone();
}
```

## Files to Modify
- src/interactive.rs: FrameBudget struct (Cell<T> fields), cpu_pressure PiApp fields, integration into view()/update()

## Dependencies
- Depends on PERF-3 (frame timing telemetry -- need Instant::now() and total_frames counter)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] FrameBudget with Cell<RenderFidelity>, Cell<u8> for counters
- [ ] cpu_pressure as plain PiApp field, sampled in update() via elapsed check
- [ ] Effective fidelity = min(frame_budget, cpu_pressure_floor)
- [ ] 3 consecutive over-budget frames trigger degradation
- [ ] 10 consecutive under-budget frames trigger recovery (with 25% hysteresis)
- [ ] Degraded mode: textwrap-only markdown, frame skipping (effective 30fps)
- [ ] Emergency mode: raw text, frame skipping (effective 15fps)
- [ ] Platform-specific load average: /proc/loadavg (Linux), libc::getloadavg (macOS), no-op (other)
- [ ] cached_view_output in RefCell<String> for frame skipping

- `tui_perf_frame_budget_normal_to_degraded` -- 3 slow frames triggers Degraded
- `tui_perf_frame_budget_degraded_to_emergency` -- sustained slow triggers Emergency
- `tui_perf_frame_budget_recovery` -- 10 fast frames triggers recovery to Normal
- `tui_perf_frame_budget_hysteresis` -- borderline frames dont oscillate
- `tui_perf_frame_budget_cell_works_in_ref` -- verify Cell<T> works through &self reference
- `tui_perf_cpu_pressure_level_calculation` -- load/cpu_count mapped correctly
- `tui_perf_effective_fidelity_combines_budget_and_pressure` -- min() logic correct
- `tui_perf_frame_skip_degraded` -- even frames skipped in Degraded mode
- `tui_perf_frame_skip_emergency` -- 3 of 4 frames skipped in Emergency mode","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:14:07.564506084Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T09:18:28.254003542Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T09:18:28.253974759Z","close_reason":"All acceptance criteria met: FrameBudget state machine with Cell<T>, CPU pressure sampling from /proc/loadavg, effective fidelity combination, 3-frame degradation threshold, 10-frame recovery, Degraded (textwrap+30fps) and Emergency (raw+15fps) modes, cached_view_output for frame skipping. 9 unit tests passing. cargo check/clippy/fmt all clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-anewk","depends_on_id":"bd-b36e3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:27:57Z","created_by":"import"}]}
